title: Demo Depot use-case manifest
description: Schema version 1 of usecase.yaml — the ONLY integration surface between a demo kit and the Demo Depot portal. Adding use case #3..N must require zero portal code changes: everything the portal needs is declared here. Top-level additionalProperties:false catches typos (PLAN.md Appendix B).

schema_version.description: Manifest schema version. Portal pins the schema it validates against.

slug.description: Stable kebab-case identifier. Used as the image name and DB key. NEVER special-cased in portal code.

story.description: Markdown. Rendered on the catalog detail page.

langfuse_features.description: Drives catalog feature filters. Free-form tags; portal renders them as chips.

project_hint.description: Portal HARD-BLOCKS deploy unless environment.project_name contains this substring, BEFORE any container starts (PLAN.md §5.2).

base_config.description: Which kit config file the portal writes as --config per host kind. v1: no YAML templating in the portal — overrides ride --set on top of the chosen base (PLAN.md §4).

default.description: Repo-relative config path used when no host-kind-specific entry matches.

config_schema.description: JSON Schema. The deploy wizard renders a form from this. v1 CONTRACT: each property NAME is a dotted kit-config key passed verbatim as `--set <name>=<value>` — no portal-side mapping table, preserving the zero-code invariant.

pipeline.description: Ordered steps. Each step becomes one Job. Missing built-in steps (e.g. no probe) simply aren't listed — the portal never assumes a command exists.

id.description: Free step identifier. Maps to jobs.kind: ids probe|plan|seed|verify|resume|teardown map to that kind; any other id (e.g. evaluators, memo) maps to kind=custom_step (PLAN.md §4).

run.description: Container command. {config} is substituted with the resolved --config path; --set flags are appended by the portal.

when.description: Simple guard expression over host_kind and config.* (e.g. "host_kind != self_hosted"). Absent = always run.

gate.description: Pause after this step for explicit user confirmation (e.g. plan event-count vs tier cap).

parse.description: Optional structured extraction from stdout. Value form: 'regex:<pattern with one capture group>'.

resumable.description: Command to re-run this step safely after failure (spool on shared volume + deterministic upsert IDs).

artifacts.description: Files the worker collects from /app/out/ after the producing step exits (PLAN.md §5.1.6).

path.description: Path relative to the container artifact dir (/app/out/).

live_components.description: Ephemeral live surfaces the portal can start as containers with a wildcard subdomain + TTL (PLAN.md §6).

command.description: Must bind 0.0.0.0 and the declared port; the portal never port-publishes directly.

requires_secrets.description: The portal injects ONLY these into the live container (least privilege, PLAN.md §9.3). Sentinel LLM_API_KEY = inject the deployment's selected provider key under that provider's canonical env name (requires a top-level `llm` block; may not be mixed with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — LAN-378).

alpha.description: Flags an experimental surface; portal renders an 'alpha' badge and may gate it.

routes.description: In-app routes this one container serves (a route is NOT a separate container). Rendered on the catalog detail page; an alpha route gets its own 'alpha' badge and may be gated independently of the component.

path.description: Route path served by this component, e.g. "/" or "/workbench".

alpha.description: Flags this route experimental even when the component is GA.

llm.description: Optional LLM-provider contract (LAN-378, additive — schema_version stays 1). Declares which LLM providers this use case supports; the portal injects the deployment's SELECTED provider key wherever a live component's requires_secrets lists the LLM_API_KEY sentinel. Manifests with a bare ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and no `llm` block stay valid and behave as an implicit `providers: [anthropic]` (back-compat). Provider id → env-var mapping lives ONLY in the portal provider table (api/app/providers.py), never here.

providers.description: Ordered list of supported provider ids; the FIRST entry is the deploy-time default. The set of valid ids mirrors the portal provider table (a drift test pins them together).

models.description: Optional per-provider model id, passed to the kit as the LLM_MODEL env var. Keys MUST be a subset of `providers` (validator-enforced, 422 otherwise).

note.description: Rendered in the teardown UX (ingestion is append-only; teardown is project-level).
